steps.title: Interpret no plateau
steps.content: The study found that zoo chimpanzees' vitamin D levels correlate with UVB irradiance and show no evidence of plateauing even at the highest local UVB levels. A plateau would suggest the animals had reached their vitamin D synthesis ceiling.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A claims mid-latitude zoo chimpanzees have vitamin D levels comparable to tropical wild chimpanzees, but the passage gives no such comparison. Choice B speculates that adaptations to seasonal UVB variation may be emerging, even though the passage says wild chimpanzees lack such adaptations.

steps.title: Use the unmet capacity
steps.content: Choice D says supplemental vitamin D would likely not help, but no plateau does not support that. Because vitamin D levels keep rising with available UVB instead of leveling off, the chimpanzees have not reached their inherent synthesis capacity under local conditions. The answer is C.
